title: Selaginella ciliata
taxonomy: Selaginella ciliata is classified under the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the division Pteridophyta, which includes vascular cryptogams. It is further categorized under the class Lycopodiopsida, a group of lycopod plants. The order Selaginellales is the next level of classification, and within this order, the family Selaginellaceae is where Selaginella ciliata is placed. The genus Selaginella is a group of spikemosses, and within this genus, Selaginella ciliata is a specific species. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Selaginella ciliata.

intro: Selaginella ciliata, also known as spiny spike moss, is a species of evergreen, vascular plant in the family Selaginellaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Selaginella ciliata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Selaginella ciliata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory problems, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
